2603.15527.title: Are Dilemmas and Conflicts in LLM Alignment Solvable? A View from Priority Graph
2603.15527.paper_summary: As Large Language Models (LLMs) become more powerful and autonomous, they increasingly face conflicts and dilemmas in many scenarios. We first summarize and taxonomize these diverse conflicts. Then, we model the LLM's preferences to make different choices as a priority graph, where instructions and values are nodes, and the edges represent context-specific priorities determined by the model's output distribution. This graph reveals that a unified stable LLM alignment is very challenging, because the graph is neither static nor necessarily consistent in different contexts. Besides, it also reveals a potential vulnerability: priority hacking, where adversaries can craft deceptive contexts to manipulate the graph and bypass safety alignments. To counter this, we propose a runtime verification mechanism, enabling LLMs to query external sources to ground their context and resist manipulation. While this approach enhances robustness, we also acknowledge that many ethical and value dilemmas are philosophically irreducible, posing a long-term, open challenge for the future of AI alignment.
2603.15527.paper_summary_zh: 隨著大型語言模型（LLMs）變得越來越強大和自主，它們在許多場景中面臨的衝突和困境也越來越多。<lb/>我們首先總結並分類這些多樣的衝突。<lb/>然後，我們將LLM的偏好建模為一個優先圖，其中指令和價值觀是節點，而邊緣則代表由模型的輸出分佈決定的特定上下文的優先級。<lb/>這個圖顯示出統一穩定的LLM對齊是非常具有挑戰性的，因為這個圖在不同上下文中既不是靜態的，也不一定是一致的。<lb/>此外，它還揭示了一個潛在的脆弱性：優先級駭客攻擊，對手可以設計欺騙性的上下文來操縱這個圖並繞過安全對齊。<lb/>為了應對這一點，我們提出了一種運行時驗證機制，使LLMs能夠查詢外部來源以確定其上下文並抵抗操縱。<lb/>雖然這種方法增強了穩健性，但我們也承認許多倫理和價值困境在哲學上是不可約簡的，這對未來的AI對齊提出了長期的、開放的挑戰。
2603.15527.author: Zhenheng Tang et.al.
2603.15527.authors: Zhenheng Tang, Xiang Liu, Qian Wang, Eunsol Choi, Bo Li, Xiaowen Chu
